description: Next-generation Grafana platform administration command center. Real-time instance health, availability, user experience, database health, and resource utilization.

panels.description: Percentage of Grafana instances reporting UP
panels.title: Platform Status

panels.description: Number of Grafana instances currently reporting as UP
panels.title: Instances Online

panels.description: Currently deployed Grafana version across instances
panels.title: Grafana Version

panels.description: Time since Grafana process started. Indicates last restart or deployment.
panels.title: Uptime

panels.description: Total inbound HTTP request rate across all Grafana instances and endpoints
panels.title: Total Request Rate

panels.description: 30-day rolling availability based on non-5xx response ratio
panels.title: Availability (30d)

panels.description: Number of active users currently tracked by the Grafana instance
panels.title: Active Users

panels.description: Total number of dashboards managed by this Grafana instance
panels.title: Total Dashboards

panels.description: HTTP request rate broken down by status code class. 5xx errors are the critical signal.
panels.title: Request Rate by Status Code

panels.description: Latency percentile distribution. P95 crossing 1.0s indicates user-visible degradation.
panels.title: Response Latency Percentiles

panels.description: Server error rate as percentage of total requests. Target: < 1%
panels.title: Error Rate %

panels.description: Top 10 slowest API endpoints by P95 latency. Focus optimization efforts here.
panels.title: Slowest Endpoints (P95)

panels.description: Top 10 endpoints with highest server error rates. These are your reliability hotspots.

targets.expr: topk(10, sum(rate(grafana_http_request_duration_seconds_count{instance=~"$instance", status_code=~"5..", handler=~"$handler"}[$__rate_interval])) by (handler) / sum(rate(grafana_http_request_duration_seconds_count{instance=~"$instance", handler=~"$handler"}[$__rate_interval])) by (handler) * 100)

panels.title: Highest Error Rate Endpoints

panels.description: Database connection pool utilization. When In Use approaches Max Open, queries will queue.
panels.title: DB Connection Pool

panels.description: Database connection pool saturation. > 80% indicates risk of connection exhaustion.
panels.title: DB Pool Saturation %

panels.description: Database connection wait events. Non-zero indicates connection pool pressure.
panels.title: DB Wait Queue

panels.description: CPU usage rate per Grafana instance. Sustained high CPU indicates compute pressure.
panels.title: CPU Usage

panels.description: Resident set size (physical memory) per Grafana instance. Trend upward may indicate a memory leak.
panels.title: Memory (RSS)

panels.description: Go runtime goroutines. Sustained increase indicates potential goroutine leak.
panels.title: Goroutines

panels.description: Go garbage collection pause time rate. Spikes correlate with memory pressure.
panels.title: GC Pause Duration

panels.description: File descriptor usage vs limit. Exhaustion causes connection failures.
panels.title: Open File Descriptors

panels.description: File descriptor saturation percentage. Above 80% risks connection failures and process instability.
panels.title: FD Saturation %

panels.description: Go heap memory breakdown. Gap between Alloc and Sys indicates fragmentation.
panels.title: Heap Memory Breakdown

title: Grafana Admin — Command Center
